LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGEMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 439'S REQUIREMENT OF A MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE. (SEE SADEGHI DEC.) COUNSEL ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE LAW. A DECLARATION MUST THEN BE FILED WHICH SPECIFIES WHETHER THE MEET AND CONFER WAS IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND THE RESPONSE TO THE IS NOW DUE BY JANUARY 19, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/RBU)

Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 439's requirement of a meet and confer in person or by telephone. (See Sadeghi Dec.) HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF LYZETTE GUTIERREZ' Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant, And Request For Sanctions First Set Of Request For Production Of Documents.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge, plaintiff, and counsel for defendants (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter called, argument/discussions are held.

At the conclusion of argument/discussions, the motion is granted as modified following argument/discussions held today. Sanctions are denied without prejudice.

The parties are to meet and confer regarding the proposed order. Mr. Tran is to prepare the form of order pursuant to CRC 3.1312 and submit it to Judge Pro Tem William Lynn at williamclynn@gmail.com. The parties may also submit the stipulated protective order to Judge Pro Tem Lynn.
